text.title: pytest Async Testing — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every python-pytest-async application must obey.

rule.statement: Set asyncio_mode = 'auto' in pyproject [tool.pytest.ini_options] for projects where most tests are async. Use 'strict' only when most tests are sync.
rule.rationale: Wrong mode silently skips test bodies (the test passes without running); explicit per-test markers are easy to forget.

rule.statement: Use @pytest_asyncio.fixture (not @pytest.fixture) for async fixtures. Yield from them like sync fixtures.
rule.rationale: @pytest.fixture on async def returns a coroutine object that is truthy; pytest treats it as a value, not an awaitable, and the test fails in obscure ways.

rule.statement: Mock async callables with AsyncMock (unittest.mock.AsyncMock or mocker.AsyncMock). Never with MagicMock.
rule.rationale: MagicMock returns a sync value when awaited (RuntimeError: was not awaited); AsyncMock returns a coroutine.

rule.statement: Test FastAPI apps via httpx.AsyncClient(transport=ASGITransport(app=app), base_url='http://test'). Do not boot the server with uvicorn for tests.
rule.rationale: ASGITransport routes requests directly to the app object — no socket, no port collision, no startup cost.

rule.statement: Never call sync Django ORM directly in an async test. Wrap in asgiref.sync.sync_to_async or write a sync test.
rule.rationale: Sync ORM blocks the event loop; the test looks like a hang and Pytest eventually times out.
